primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison by phosphorylation, acetylation, ubiquitination, or activation markers.
evidence: This revealed details of the structural differences and similarities between C3(H2O) and C3, as well as between C3(H2O) and its pivotal proteolytic cleavage product, C3b
Quantitative comparison of the cross-linking results obtained for two different conformations of a protein allows the details of the conformational change to be elucidated.
C3(H2O) (i.e. C3(N) in this case but identical to C3(H2O)) was prepared by incubating C3 at 37 °C with 200 mm methylamine (CH3NH2) at pH 8.3 for three hours.

replicate_hints: C3, C3b and C3(H2O) samples were prepared in two separated batched and used for “experiment I” and “experiment II” respectively.
Only those cross-links that were consistently quantified in both paired replicas (i.e. with label-swapping) were accepted
multiplex_channel_hints: bis[sulfosuccinimidyl] suberate (BS3) ... or its deuterated analogue bis[sulfosuccinimidyl] 2,2,7,7-suberate-d4 (BS3-d4)
equimolar quantities of the tryptic products from the six cross-linked protein samples were mixed pair-wise in four combinations

evidence: C3(H2O) Versus C3 Comparison Confirms Structural Rearrangements in C3(H2O)
C3(H2O) Versus C3b Comparison Confirms Similar Domain Architectures

quantification_strategy: Relative quantification of light and heavy signals

targets_and_enrichments.ontology_label: Chemical cross-linking coupled with mass spectrometry proteomics

base_candidates_ranked.reason: Data-dependent acquisition (DDA) was used on an Orbitrap instrument. While isotope labeling (BS3/BS3-d4) was used for quantitation, the final analysis reported signal fold-changes and median normalization, which is functionally similar to LFQ-style processing of labeled pairs.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment is explicitly described as Quantitative Cross-Linking/Mass Spectrometry (QCLMS) using BS3 and BS3-d4 cross-linkers.
